page: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server based on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of system memory. The CPU architecture is x86_64, and the instance includes 20 GB of local SSD storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-threaded and multi-threaded execution. Due to its single-core configuration, the server achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and high throughput in single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. However, multi-core benchmarks, PassMark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth tests fall into the weak or poor categories, reflecting the shared-core allocation and limited memory capacity.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a budget-friendly tier designed for low-resource workloads. The trade-off involves high single-core burst capability against constrained memory bandwidth and limited multi-threaded capacity. It is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development environments, or simple background tasks that do not require sustained multi-core processing.
description: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with a single shared vCPU from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 20 GB of local SSD storage. While its single-core performance is highly competitive, achieving top-tier results in single-threaded compression and stress-ng tests, its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities are limited due to the single shared-core architecture. This resource profile makes it a cost-effective option for lightweight workloads. The instance also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address, making it immediately accessible for network-based testing.
